ingredients: For Ugali: 4 cups water, 2 cups white maize flour.
For Sukuma Wiki: 1 bunch collard greens (finely sliced), 1 onion (diced), 2 tomatoes (chopped), 2 tbsp oil, salt to taste.
steps: Ugali: Bring water to a boil in a heavy-bottomed pot.
Gradually add maize flour while stirring vigorously with a wooden spoon (mwiko) to avoid lumps.
Keep adding flour and mashing against the sides until it reaches a thick, dough-like consistency.
Cover and let it "bake" on low heat for 5 minutes.
Sukuma Wiki: Heat oil in a pan and sauté the onions until translucent.
Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Toss in the sliced greens and a pinch of salt.
Stir and cook for only 5–7 minutes to keep them vibrant and slightly crunchy.
Serve: Form the Ugali into a mound and serve hot alongside the greens.

ingredients: 500g beef (cubed), 2 cups Basmati rice, 2 onions (thinly sliced),
1 tbsp Pilau Masala (cumin, cloves, cardamom, cinnamon, black pepper).
steps: Boil the beef until tender; set aside the broth.
In a large pot, heat oil and fry onions until they are dark brown (this gives the rice its color).
Add ginger-garlic paste, the Pilau Masala spices, and the beef.
Stir for 2 minutes.Add the potatoes and the rice. Stir to coat the grains in oil and spices.
Add 4 cups of liquid (beef broth + water).
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and cover tightly.Simmer for about 20 minutes until the water is absorbed and the rice is fluffy.

ingredients: Meat: 1kg goat or beef ribs/chunks, coarse salt.
Kachumbari: 2 tomatoes (diced), 1 red onion (thinly sliced), fresh coriander (diced), 1 chili (optional), lemon juice, salt.
steps: Meat: Season the meat generously with salt.
Grill over hot charcoal (medium-low heat) for 30–40 minutes, turning occasionally until charred on the outside and juicy inside.
Kachumbari: Soak the sliced onions in salty water for 10 minutes to remove the "sting," then drain.
Mix with the tomatoes, coriander, chili, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt.
Serve: Slice the meat into bite-sized pieces and serve with the fresh salad and a side of Ugali.

ingredients: 2 potatoes (cubed), 1 tsp curry powder, salt, oil.
steps: Sauté the onions in oil until golden. Add tomatoes and cook until they form a thick paste.
Add the potatoes, curry powder, and a splash of water. Cover and cook until potatoes are halfway done.
Add the pre-boiled maize and beans. Stir well to combine.
Add just enough water to cover the mixture and simmer on low heat for 15 minutes until the flavors meld and the liquid thickens into a light gravy.

ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp sugar (optional), 1 cup warm water, vegetable oil.
steps: Mix flour, salt, sugar, and 2 tbsp oil.
Gradually add warm water and knead for 10 minutes until you have a soft, non-sticky dough.
Let it rest for 20 minutes.Divide into balls. Roll a ball into a flat circle, brush with oil, and roll it up like a rope.
Coil that rope into a "snail" shape.Roll the "snail" flat again into a circle.
Fry on a hot pan (without oil first) for 1 minute each side. Then add a teaspoon of oil under the chapati and fry until golden brown spots appear on both sides.

ingredients: For the eggs: 3 eggs (beaten), 1 onion (finely chopped), 2 tomatoes (pureed or finely diced), 1 green chili (optional), 1/2 tsp turmeric (optional for color), salt, 2 tbsp oil.
For the Ugali: 2 cups maize flour, 3 cups water.
steps: The Eggs: Heat oil in a pan and sauté onions until softened. Add the tomatoes and spices. Cook until the tomatoes turn into a thick, oily paste.

Pour the beaten eggs into the tomato mixture. Let them sit for a minute to set slightly, then scramble gently so you have large, juicy chunks of egg coated in the tomato base. Do not overdry the eggs.
The Ugali: Bring water to a boil. Gradually add maize flour while stirring and mashing with a wooden spoon until it forms a stiff, smooth cake. Let it steam for 5 minutes.
Serve: Cut the hot Ugali into wedges and serve it immediately with the "juicy" eggs on top.

ingredients: 1kg pork ribs (cut into individual ribs), 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp honey (optional), 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp cayenne pepper, salt to taste, 2 tbsp oil.
steps: Instructions
The Marinade: Mix the soy sauce, honey, ginger-garlic paste, cayenne, and salt. Coat the ribs thoroughly and let them marinate for at least 30 minutes.
The Searing: Heat oil in a heavy pan. Sear the ribs on high heat for 2-3 minutes per side until browned.

The Slow Cook: Add half a cup of water to the pan, cover tightly with a lid, and reduce the heat to low. Let the ribs steam-cook in their own juices for 25–30 minutes until the water is gone and the meat is tender.
The Finish: Turn up the heat for the last 5 minutes to caramelize the remaining juices onto the ribs until they are sticky and dark brown. Serve with a side of Ugali or chips.

ingredients: 6 medium potatoes (peeled and boiled until just tender), 1 cup gram flour (besan) or all-purpose flour, 1 tsp turmeric powder (for that bright yellow color), 1 tsp chili powder, salt, water, oil for deep frying.
steps: Prep: Cut the boiled potatoes into quarters or thick slices.
The Batter: In a bowl, mix the flour, turmeric, chili powder, and salt. Gradually add water and whisk until you have a thick, smooth batter (similar to pancake batter).
The Fry: Heat oil in a deep pan. Dip each potato piece into the yellow batter, ensuring it’s fully coated, then carefully drop it into the hot oil.
Finish: Fry for 3–5 minutes until the outside is crispy.
Pro Tip: Serve these hot with a side of Ukwaju (tamarind sauce) or a spicy tomato chutney.
